channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas brushes off Trump's threat and says it will only free hostages in return for a lasting truce
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The Hamas militant group on Thursday brushed off President Donald Trump's latest threat and reiterated that it will only free the remaining Israeli hostages in exchange for a lasting ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France steps in to provide military intelligence to Ukraine as the U.S. freezes vital information
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France is providing military intelligence to Ukraine after Washington announced it was freezing the sharing of information with Kyiv, French defense Minister Sebastien Lecornu said Thursday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France steps in to provide military intelligence to Ukraine as the U.S. freezes vital information
item.description: French defense Minister Sebastien Lecornu says France is providing military intelligence to Ukraine after Washington announced it was freezing the sharing of information with Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean fighter jets accidentally drop bombs during training exercise, injuring multiple people
item.description: A South Korean fighter jet accidentally dropped bombs on a civilian area during a training exercise Thursday, injuring more than a dozen people, according to local news reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU leaders open emergency summit on defense and Ukraine aid as US security support wanes
item.description: European Union leaders are holding emergency summit talks to beef up their own military defenses and make sure Ukraine will still be properly protected by its allies.
item.dc:creator: Raf Casert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Women's rights are under attack 30 years after leaders adopted a blueprint for equality, UN says
item.description: Thirty years after world leaders adopted a historic blueprint to achieve gender equality, a new United Nations report says women’s and girls’ rights are under attack and gender discrimination is deeply embedded in economies and societies.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Clutched by the global faithful and draped on candles and statues, rosaries abound for Pope Francis
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — As Pope Francis remains hospitalized in Rome, thousands of faithful from around the world have been gathering by St. Peter’s and by the hospital to pray the rosary for him.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schools are closed and public transport has stopped as rare cyclone approaches Australian coast
item.description: Schools are closed, public transport has stopped and desperate residents are getting around shortages of sandbags by buying sacks of potting mix as a region of eastern Australia is lashed by wind and rain from a rare approaching tropical cyclone.
item.dc:creator: John Pye And Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An Okinawan bone digger searches for remains from one of the fiercest battles of World War II
item.description: Takamatsu Gushiken hunts caves in Okinawa's jungles for the bones of those who died in the WWII Battle of Okinawa, one of the war's bloodiest.
item.dc:creator: Ayaka Mcgill, Hiro Komae And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Protests turn violent as Greece's government battles censure motion over deadly train crash
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Protesters launched gasoline bombs and fireworks in clashes with police outside Greece's parliament late Wednesday, in renewed nationwide protests calling for politicians to be held accountable for a 2023 rail disaster that claimed 57 lives.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS and THANASSIS STAVRAKIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nearly a dozen students found dismembered by Mexico highway after vanishing on vacation
item.description: Authorities are investigating after nine bodies were found on Sunday near an abandoned car in Mexico. Local reports note the bodies belonged to missing students.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Majority say US, not other countries, will feel brunt of Trump tariffs: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans in a new poll said that the U.S. and not other countries will feel the brunt of President Trump’s tariffs. When asked about “the cost of tariffs on foreign products imported into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Vance suggests other industries won't get tariff carve-outs
item.description: Vice President Vance on Wednesday said that industries other than U.S. automakers have asked for exemptions on tariffs, but suggested that the Trump administration would not extend further carve outs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: The pope has increased his physical therapy and marked the start of Lent in the hospital
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis increased his physical therapy with his condition remaining stable on Wednesday, marking the start of Lent by receiving ashes on his forehead and calling the parish priest in Gaza, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump issues 'last warning' to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza
item.description: President Donald Trump has issued what he calls a “last warning” to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pentagon denies reports Hegseth ordered halt in cyber operations against Russia
item.description: The Department of Defense is denying reports Secretary Pete Hegseth halted offensive cyber operations against Russia.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Trump administration pauses flow of intelligence to Ukraine that helps on battlefield
item.description: The U.S. has paused its intelligence sharing with Ukraine, cutting off the flow of vital information that has helped Kyiv defend against Russian invaders.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper And Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance: 'Absurdly dishonest' to suggest he criticized British, French troops
item.description: Vice President Vance is pushing back on claims that he criticized British and French troops during a Monday interview.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: China sticks to an economic growth target of 'around 5%' despite a looming trade war with US
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — The Chinese government unveiled an annual economic growth target of “around 5%” on Wednesday, despite the possible negative impact of a looming trade war with the United States, and pledged to address what it called “sluggish” consumer spending at home.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU and KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China will increase its defense budget 7.2% this year
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Wednesday it will increase its defense budget 7.2% this year, as it continues its campaign to build a larger, more modern military to assert its territorial claims and challenge the U.S. defense lead in Asia.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New Zealand lawmakers told to stop complaining about use of the country's Māori name in Parliament
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — The speaker of New Zealand ’s Parliament told lawmakers he would not consider further complaints about the use of the country’s Māori name, Aotearoa, in Parliament, after one lawmaker sought to have it banned.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Houthis designated as foreign terrorist organization, Rubio says
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Tuesday that the Iran-backed Houthis in Yemen are being designated as a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: With the Gaza ceasefire in limbo, Israel tries to impose an alternative plan on Hamas
item.description: Israel this week introduced what it said was a new U.S. ceasefire plan — different from the one it agreed to in January — and is trying to force Hamas to accept it by imposing a siege on the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump’s row with Zelenskyy recalls Obama, Biden humiliations of Netanyahu
item.description: The clash between President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodmyr Zelenskyy has led analysts to recall the treatment meted out by the Obama and Biden administrations against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump again attacks Zelenskyy with potential peace deal in limbo
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Israel is criticized after it bars Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel faced sharp criticism as it stopped the entry of all food and other supplies into Gaza on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” for Hamas if a fragile ceasefire isn't extended.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Francis off mechanical ventilation entirely, signaling he's overcome risks from respiratory crisis
item.description: Pope Francis said he was living his hospitalization as an experience of profound solidarity with people who are sick and suffering everywhere.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the United States, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as European leaders assembled in London to discuss ending the war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis off mechanical ventilator, has no fever or signs of infection: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is “still resting following a peaceful night" as he continues his recovery from double pneumonia, the Vatican confirmed early Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis stable a day after respiratory crisis, spent 'long periods' off noninvasive ventilator
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition Saturday a day after a respiratory crisis during his battle with double pneumonia, the Vatican said. He spent long periods of the day off the noninvasive mechanical ventilation he initially needed in a sign that his lung function was improving.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope had bronchial spasm, resulted in him inhaling vomit, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
